name: Naturepedia Soil Systems Knowledge Mesh
description: Paid relationship mesh connecting soil systems, water systems, microbial life, plant intelligence, mycorrhizal networks, nutrient cycling, carbon cycle, ecosystem feedbacks, and regenerative soil systems.
architecture: Soil Systems → Water Systems → Microbial Life → Mycorrhizal Networks → Plant Intelligence → Nutrient Cycling → Carbon Cycle → Ecosystem Feedbacks → Regenerative Soil Systems

relationshipMesh.relationship: Water shapes soil formation, infiltration, erosion, groundwater recharge, nutrient movement, microbial habitat, drought buffering, and plant-available moisture.
Soils host bacteria, archaea, fungi, biofilms, decomposers, nutrient cyclers, and microbial communities that drive living soil function.
Microbial communities transform minerals, organic matter, nitrogen, phosphorus, carbon compounds, and root exudates into biologically available pathways.
Fungi and microbes connect roots, minerals, water, carbon compounds, warning signals, and underground resource exchange.
Plant-fungal networks support sensing, cooperation, stress response, defense signaling, root intelligence, and belowground ecological communication.
Plants feed soil life through root exudates, litter, carbon compounds, and microbial partnerships that regulate nutrient access.
Nutrient cycling controls decomposition, photosynthesis support, microbial biomass, root growth, soil organic matter, and carbon stabilization.
Soils store and stabilize carbon through organic matter, aggregates, roots, fungal networks, microbial residues, moisture, and mineral interactions.
Soil carbon influences climate regulation, fertility, moisture retention, biodiversity, plant growth, microbial activity, and resilience feedback loops.
Regenerative soil systems strengthen Earth system resilience through carbon storage, water retention, biodiversity recovery, nutrient cycling, and living feedback pathways.
